testModifications.reasoning: The existing tests in precheck-checks.test.ts were updated to pass the new workdir parameter to checkOptionalCommands(). This is a legitimate fix required by the new function signature. The changes only update function calls to match the new API; no assertions were loosened or removed. All 72 tests in both test files pass successfully.

criteria.criterion: checkOptionalCommands() resolves typecheck via: execution.typecheckCommand -> review.commands.typecheck -> package.json typecheck script
criteria.notes: Implementation at src/precheck/checks-warnings.ts:121-147 correctly implements priority chain with checks at lines 130-131 (execution), 135-136 (review.commands), and 140-142 (package.json)

criteria.criterion: checkOptionalCommands() resolves lint via: execution.lintCommand -> review.commands.lint -> package.json lint script
criteria.notes: Same resolveCommandForPrecheck() function handles both 'lint' and 'typecheck' command types via parameter. Tested extensively in test/unit/precheck/checks-warnings.test.ts

criteria.criterion: If config.review.commands.typecheck is set, precheck passes with no warning
criteria.notes: Test case 'passes when review.commands.typecheck and review.commands.lint are set' confirms this behavior

criteria.criterion: If neither execution field, review.commands, nor package.json script exists, precheck still warns 'not configured'
criteria.notes: Test case 'warns with both lint and typecheck when nothing is configured' confirms this behavior with expected warning message

criteria.criterion: Unit tests: config with review.commands.typecheck set -> check passes; config with neither -> check warns; config with package.json script -> check passes
criteria.notes: All three scenarios are covered: test case 'passes when review.commands.typecheck...is set', 'warns with both lint and typecheck...', and 'passes when package.json has both typecheck and lint scripts'

reasoning: All 14 checkOptionalCommands-related tests pass (11 dedicated tests + 3 integration tests). The implementation correctly mirrors the priority chain used in review/runner.ts:resolveCommand(). The new resolveCommandForPrecheck() function properly handles all three priority levels (execution config, review.commands, and package.json). Helper functions loadPackageJsonForPrecheck() and hasScriptForPrecheck() are clean and focused. Test file size (220 lines) and implementation file size (207 lines) are well under the 400-line project convention limit. No console.log statements or other code quality issues detected. The test modifications in precheck-checks.test.ts are legitimate - they only update function calls to pass the new workdir parameter required by the updated function signature. The implementation is ready for production.
